heading: Duties of Secretary relating to nonprocurement debarment
label: 1769f Duties of Secretary relating to nonprocurement debarment

children.text: establishing guidelines and a timetable for the Secretary to initiate debarment proceedings, as well as establishing mandatory debarment periods; and
providing training, technical advice, and guidance in identifying and preventing the activities.

children.label: A
children.text: the school lunch program established under this chapter;

children.label: B
children.text: the summer food service program for children established under ;section 1761 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: the child and adult care food program established under ;section 1766 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: the special milk program established under ;section 1772 of this title

children.label: E
children.text: the school breakfast program established under ; andsection 1773 of this title

children.label: F
children.text: the special supplemental nutrition program for women, infants, and children authorized under .section 1786 of this title

children.heading: Contractor
children.label: 2 Contractor
children.text: The term “contractor” means a person that contracts with a State, an agency of a State, or a local agency to provide goods or services in relation to the participation of a local agency in a child nutrition program.

children.heading: Local agency
children.label: 3 Local agency
children.text: The term “local agency” means a school, school food authority, child care center, sponsoring organization, or other entity authorized to operate a child nutrition program at the local level.

children.heading: Nonprocurement debarment
children.label: 4 Nonprocurement debarment
children.text: The term “nonprocurement debarment” means an action to bar a person from programs and activities involving Federal financial and nonfinancial assistance, but not including Federal procurement programs and activities.

children.heading: Person
children.label: 5 Person
children.text: The term “person” means any individual, corporation, partnership, association, cooperative, or other legal entity, however organized.

children.heading: Assistance to identify and prevent fraud and anticompetitive activities
children.label: c Assistance to identify and prevent fraud and anticompetitive activities

children.text: in cooperation with any other appropriate individual, organization, or agency, provide advice, training, technical assistance, and guidance (which may include awareness training, training films, and troubleshooting advice) to representatives of States and local agencies regarding means of identifying and preventing fraud and anticompetitive activities relating to the provision of goods or services in conjunction with the participation of a local agency in a child nutrition program; and

provide information to, and fully cooperate with, the Attorney General and State attorneys general regarding investigations of fraud and anticompetitive activities relating to the provision of goods or services in conjunction with the participation of a local agency in a child nutrition program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (3) and subsection (e), not later than 180 days after notification of the occurrence of a cause for debarment described in paragraph (2), the Secretary shall initiate nonprocurement debarment proceedings against the contractor who has committed the cause for debarment.

children.label: A
children.text: an anticompetitive activity, including bid-rigging, price-fixing, the allocation of customers between competitors, or other violation of Federal or State antitrust laws;

children.label: B
children.text: fraud, bribery, theft, forgery, or embezzlement;

children.label: C
children.text: knowingly receiving stolen property;

children.label: D
children.text: making a false claim or statement; or

children.label: E
children.text: any other obstruction of justice.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 3 Exception
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a decision on initiating nonprocurement debarment proceedings cannot be made within 180 days after notification of the occurrence of a cause for debarment described in paragraph (2) because of the need to further investigate matters relating to the possible debarment, the Secretary may have such additional time as the Secretary considers necessary to make a decision, but not to exceed an additional 180 days.

children.heading: Mandatory child nutrition program debarment periods
children.label: 4 Mandatory child nutrition program debarment periods

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to the other provisions of this paragraph and notwithstanding any other provision of law except subsection (e), if, after deciding to initiate nonprocurement debarment proceedings pursuant to paragraph (1), the Secretary decides to debar a contractor, the debarment shall be for a period of not less than 3 years.

children.heading: Previous debarment
children.label: B Previous debarment
children.text: If the contractor has been previously debarred pursuant to nonprocurement debarment proceedings initiated pursuant to paragraph (1), and the cause for debarment is described in paragraph (2) based on activities that occurred subsequent to the initial debarment, the debarment shall be for a period of not less than 5 years.

children.heading: Scope
children.label: C Scope
children.text: At a minimum, a debarment under this subsection shall serve to bar the contractor for the specified period from contracting to provide goods or services in conjunction with the participation of a local agency in a child nutrition program.

children.label: i
children.text: reverse a debarment decision;

children.label: ii
children.text: reduce the period or scope of a debarment;

children.label: iii
children.text: grant an exception permitting a debarred contractor to participate in a particular contract to provide goods or services; or

children.label: iv
children.text: otherwise settle a debarment action at any time;

children.heading: Information
children.label: 5 Information
children.text: On request, the Secretary shall present to the Committee on Education and Labor, and the Committee on Agriculture, of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate information regarding the decisions required by this subsection.

children.heading: Relationship to other authorities
children.label: 6 Relationship to other authorities
children.text: A debarment imposed under this section shall not reduce or diminish the authority of a Federal, State, or local government agency or court to penalize, imprison, fine, suspend, debar, or take other adverse action against a person in a civil, criminal, or administrative proceeding.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 7 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall issue such regulations as are necessary to carry out this subsection.
is likely to have a significant adverse effect on competition or prices in the relevant market or nationally;
will interfere with the ability of a local agency to procure a needed product for a child nutrition program;

is unfair to a person, subsidiary corporation, affiliate, parent company, or local division of a corporation that is not involved in the improper activity that would otherwise result in the debarment;
is likely to have significant adverse economic impacts on the local economy in a manner that is unfair to innocent parties;

is not justified in light of the penalties already imposed on the contractor for violations relevant to the proposed debarment, including any suspension or debarment arising out of the same matter that is imposed by any Federal or State agency; or
is not in the public interest, or otherwise is not in the interests of justice, as determined by the Secretary.
exhaust all administrative procedures prescribed by the Secretary; and
receive notice of the final determination of the Secretary.

children.heading: Information relating to prevention and control of anticompetitive activities
children.label: g Information relating to prevention and control of anticompetitive activities
children.text: On request, the Secretary shall present to the Committee on Education and Labor, and the Committee on Agriculture, of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate information regarding the activities of the Secretary relating to anticompetitive activities, fraud, nonprocurement debarment, and any waiver granted by the Secretary under this section.
